$comment: Frozen snapshot of boundary-test.sh's output at the start of the Layer 2 refactor. Each migration step in docs/investigations/G-migration.md should drive `total_violations` monotonically toward zero. Step 6 flips the CI gate to failing once the count hits 0.

not_banned_but_in_audit.$comment: A's audit reported '16 src files touching @earendil-works/*'. That total includes @earendil-works/pi-ai imports (the model provider). pi-ai is NOT banned — it's a peer dependency the agent driver retains. The 21/14 baseline below counts only the banned packages.

notes: All 21 violations are from the @earendil-works/pi-* SDK family (excluding pi-ai). The @inbrowser/* family is already clean in src/ — it only appears in examples/browser/, which is out of scope for the boundary check today (added in a later step).

src/operations/*.ts each import exactly one type alias (e.g. ReadOperations). These are the most mechanical to migrate (Step 2). src/sandbox.ts, src/tools.ts, src/session.ts are heavier and migrate in Step 5.
Reproduce: `git checkout 0ebd665 && bash scripts/boundary-test.sh`. Or on any branch: compare output of `bash scripts/boundary-test.sh` against this JSON.
